path = "/mnt/data/file-GzC7s7DW6NwdZjxze7hp8s-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:5000]

import re, json, textwrap, sys, pprint
text = open("/mnt/data/file-GzC7s7DW6NwdZjxze7hp8s-text_to_analyse.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()
# Find all occurrences of { ... } braces and capture inside.
# But second missing closing brace; so we handle open brace occurrences and extract content until closing brace if exists, else until end of line? or end of file.
positions = []
for m in re.finditer(r'\{', text):
    start_brace = m.start()
    # Seek next closing brace after start
    close = text.find('}', start_brace+1)
    if close == -1:
        # No closing brace; take until end of text
        inner = text[start_brace+1:]
        inner_end = len(text)
    else:
        inner = text[start_brace+1:close]
        inner_end = close
    # Trim leading/trailing whitespace per rule 2
    inner_stripped = inner.strip()
    # compute adjusted start and end
    # find first index of stripped inside inner
    leading_ws_len = len(inner) - len(inner.lstrip())
    trailing_ws_len = len(inner) - len(inner.rstrip())
    start_index = start_brace + 1 + leading_ws_len
    end_index = inner_end - trailing_ws_len  # exclusive end
    positions.append((start_index, end_index, text[start_index:end_index]))
positions, [text[s:e] for s,e,_ in positions]
